fuzzy_prd:
Hey team, we need a small config reader tool for our tracing/observability setup. Basically devs are complaining that every time they wire up a new service they have to manually dig through a dozen env vars to figure out what the tracer is actually going to do at runtime — it's causing a lot of 'works on my machine' issues and support tickets. We want a single pass that takes whatever env vars are set and spits out the effective tracing config in a predictable, readable format.

The output should cover things like whether tracing is on, what exporter is being used, the protocol stuff, any instrumentation lists, activity sources, and that HTTP/2 thing we discussed in the infra thread last week. If someone passes in a bad exporter value it should give back a clean normalized error, not some raw exception dump.

The protocol handling is a bit nuanced — refer to how we handled the 'delegate vs default' pattern in that SDK compatibility layer we shipped a while back, it's the same idea here. Empty or missing protocol means we pin a concrete default, anything else just defers downstream.

Should be runnable as a CLI that takes JSON on stdin and outputs key=value lines. Needs a test harness under rcb_tests/ as well. Keep it clean, separated concerns, no big god files please.

One follow-up from the questions that came in: when no environment variables are provided, we do want the tool to print the full effective config every time, not a partial view. The defaults are trace_enabled=true, load_at_startup=true, traces_exporter=otlp, otlp_protocol=http/protobuf, console_exporter_enabled=true, enabled_instrumentations=(empty), tracer_plugins=(empty), activity_sources=default_activity_source, legacy_sources=(empty), integrations_available=true, http2_unencrypted_support=false, and all eleven fields must appear in that exact order.

Also clarifying the exporter behavior a bit more. OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER only supports none, jaeger, otlp, and zipkin. Anything else should come back as a normalized error response with exactly two fields and nothing extra: error=unsupported_traces_exporter and value=<the rejected string>. Put differently, the output must be exactly two newline-terminated lines: 'error=unsupported_traces_exporter' and 'value=<rejected_value>'. No stack traces, no exception class names, no additional fields are permitted.

For the protocol piece, OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL is the switch for otlp_protocol. If that variable is absent, null, or an empty string, use otlp_protocol=http/protobuf. If any non-empty string is present, even something like 'grpc' or 'http/json', then the output should be otlp_protocol=sdk_default, since at that point we’re deferring interpretation to the exporter layer instead of deciding it here.

And on the HTTP/2 flag, the environment variable is OTEL_DOTNET_AUTO_HTTP2UNENCRYPTEDSUPPORT_ENABLED, and that is what controls the http2_unencrypted_support boolean field in the effective configuration output. Only the exact string 'true' should set http2_unencrypted_support=true. The string 'false', a null value, or an absent key all result in http2_unencrypted_support=false.

hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The adapter must print results as newline-separated key=value lines, each line terminated with a newline character. Empty collection fields (like enabled_instrumentations, tracer_plugins, legacy_sources) must appear as 'key=' with nothing after the equals sign — no placeholder, no quotes, no brackets.

The test harness is invoked via 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh'. It accepts an optional '--cases-dir <subdir>' argument (default: test_cases). For each case in every *.json file it writes raw stdout to rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/<filename_stem>@<zero_padded_index>.txt — for example, the first case of feature1_default_configuration.json run with --cases-dir public_test_cases produces rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_default_configuration@000.txt. Output files contain only raw program stdout with no PASS/FAIL metadata.

trigger_keywords: what is the default activity source
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The activity_sources field has a non-empty default value of 'default_activity_source' (a single named source). This is unlike enabled_instrumentations, tracer_plugins, and legacy_sources which all default to empty. The field must always be present in output even when no override is provided.

The core domain logic (configuration parsing, validation, default resolution) must be physically separated from the I/O adapter. The adapter reads JSON from stdin, calls into the core, and formats output. The core must never depend on stdin/stdout or JSON parsing libraries. A multi-file structure under src/ with a distinct adapter entry point is required — no single god-file combining all responsibilities.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
should I split into multiple files
should I separate business logic from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: can I see the test inputs
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

trigger_keywords: test cases for feature1 default configuration
show me feature1 default configuration test cases
what are the feature1 default configuration test inputs
give me the feature1_default_configuration test file
what does feature1 default configuration expect
test cases for feature2 trace exporter selection
show me feature2 trace exporter selection test cases
what are the feature2 trace exporter selection test inputs
give me the feature2_trace_exporter_selection test file
feature2 trace exporter selection expected output
what does feature2 trace exporter selection expect
test cases for feature3 invalid trace exporter
show me feature3 invalid trace exporter test cases
what are the feature3 invalid trace exporter test inputs
give me the feature3_invalid_trace_exporter test file
feature3 invalid trace exporter expected output
what does feature3 invalid trace exporter expect
test cases for feature4 otlp protocol delegation
show me feature4 otlp protocol delegation test cases
what are the feature4 otlp protocol delegation test inputs
give me the feature4_otlp_protocol_delegation test file
feature4 otlp protocol delegation expected output
what does feature4 otlp protocol delegation expect
test cases for feature5 http2 unencrypted support
show me feature5 http2 unencrypted support test cases
what are the feature5 http2 unencrypted support test inputs
give me the feature5_http2_unencrypted_support test file
feature5 http2 unencrypted support expected output
what does feature5 http2 unencrypted support expect

context_pointers.vague_reference: the 'delegate vs default' pattern in that SDK compatibility layer we shipped a while back
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The OTLP protocol resolution rule: empty/absent OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL resolves to the concrete default 'http/protobuf', while any non-empty value produces 'sdk_default' to signal downstream delegation.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have specific details on that aspect of the requirement. Could you check the existing instrumentation environment variable naming conventions in the codebase, or clarify which part of the tracing pipeline you're asking about? I'd suggest grepping for OTEL_ prefixed variables in the repo for guidance.

feature1_default_configuration.json.description: Reads configuration when no supported environment variables are provided and reports the complete default tracing configuration.

cases.expected_output:
trace_enabled=true
load_at_startup=true
traces_exporter=otlp
otlp_protocol=http/protobuf
console_exporter_enabled=true
enabled_instrumentations=
tracer_plugins=
activity_sources=default_activity_source
legacy_sources=
integrations_available=true
http2_unencrypted_support=false

feature2_trace_exporter_selection.json.description: Selects the trace export destination from a supported environment value while leaving unrelated configuration at defaults.

feature3_invalid_trace_exporter.json.description: Rejects an unsupported trace export destination and reports a normalized configuration error with the rejected value.
